mechanism_narrative: Positive momentum in retail sales raises expected near-term revenues for cyclical and consumer-facing firms, so a rising 3-month change in RSAFS predicts positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if periods with positive 3-month RSAFS growth do not have higher mean 20-day S&P 500 returns than periods with negative 3-month RSAFS growth over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS crosses above 3.0%, tighter short rates raise equity discount rates and reduce valuation multiples, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered FEDFUNDS-above-3.0% observations produce positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or have nonnegative average forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE through 4.8% signals weakening labor demand and rising recession risk, reducing expected corporate earnings and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE crosses above 4.8% after being at or below 4.8% in the prior 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if UNRATE cross-above-4.8% triggers are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of walk-forward cases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates improving real output and operating leverage for listed firms, so positive 3-month INDPRO momentum predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if positive 3-month INDPRO momentum has a walk-forward Rank IC below 0.03 for 20-day S&P 500 returns over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Very high HOUST combined with elevated FEDFUNDS indicates housing overextension facing tighter financing, which weakens future construction demand and credit conditions, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint HOUST > 2000 and FEDFUNDS > 3.0% state does not underperform other states in average 60-day S&P 500 returns by at least 100 basis points.

mechanism_narrative: PAYEMS expansion while UNRATE remains below 5.0% signals broad income growth without visible labor-market stress, supporting consumption and earnings, and producing positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint PAYEMS-up and UNRATE-below-5.0% condition has nonpositive average 20-day S&P 500 returns or underperforms unconditional returns in rolling tests.

mechanism_narrative: Positive real GDP momentum raises expected aggregate earnings growth and reduces macro default risk, so an increasing GDPC1 level relative to the prior quarter predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if positive quarter-over-quarter GDPC1 growth fails to produce higher 60-day S&P 500 returns than nonpositive GDPC1 growth in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating CPIAUCSL while FEDFUNDS is flat or rising signals persistent inflation pressure that forces restrictive policy and valuation compression, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized growth exceeds its 12-month growth and FEDFUNDS 3-month change >= 0
failure_condition: Reject if this inflation-acceleration and non-easing policy trigger has positive average 5-day S&P 500 returns or a hit rate below 52% for negative returns.
